root_cause: UMA is a multi-task model conditioned on a PER-GRAPH global charge/spin embedding (intensive). E(host+Ar) - E(host) - E(Ar) is evaluated across THREE separate graphs, so the per-graph global conditioning term does not cancel even at infinite separation -> a task-dependent ~120-470 kJ/mol offset. Naive test-particle (Widom) differencing is ill-defined for conditioned multi-task potentials.
implication: The min_U=+134 / REFUSE verdict from the first run is contaminated by this offset and is NOT reported as a physics result.
governance_value: The SAME Gate-1 check that PASSED for MACE-MPA-0 (U_far=0.000) FAILED for UMA (|U_far|>>0). Gate 1 discriminates not just models but whether the harness itself is valid for a given engine — and prevented a false UMA claim.
fix_path: Use a single-graph adsorption protocol for UMA (score the full host+guest system with a consistent in-graph reference, e.g. relaxed-vs-inserted within one graph), or subtract UMA's per-graph reference explicitly. Not a drop-in ASE-calculator differencing.
note: ODAC25 (esen-sm-full-odac25) still gated (separate facebook/ODAC25 license); UMA includes the odac task head and was the accessible deployment-class model.
